vBRIEFInfo.description: Add a standalone brownfield adoption guide (docs/BROWNFIELD.md) for existing projects adding Deft.

plan.title: docs(readme): missing brownfield adoption guide for existing projects

narratives.Problem: Deft has no guidance for users adding it to an existing project. The greenfield path is covered, but brownfield users (existing codebase, existing SPECIFICATION.md/PROJECT.md, pre-v0.20 layouts) have no documentation for installation options, the `task migrate:vbrief` flow, how .md files become rendered artifacts, what the pre-cutover detection guard does on first setup, and how to preserve existing spec content via the #397 ingestion path.
narratives.Action: Create a standalone docs/BROWNFIELD.md guide with sections: (1) Install options (submodule / installer / direct clone); (2) task migrate:vbrief flow (idempotent, what it parses, what it creates, what it replaces); (3) What changes after migration -- .vbrief.json as source of truth, .md as rendered views, deprecation sentinels; (4) Pre-cutover detection guard explanation (criteria, action) + CLI path (#410 gate); (5) Post-migration checklist (task vbrief:validate, task check, renders, commit); (6) Troubleshooting for common failure modes; (7) Related reading. Per charter: standalone file, no separate README section; referenced from QUICK-START.md Step 2 Case C and Step 3 note. A brief 'Brownfield adoption' pointer from README Step 4 gives users a reachable entry point without duplicating content. Do not link to UPGRADING.md yet (branch 4) -- mention by name only.
narratives.Constraint: Charter directive: 'standalone docs/BROWNFIELD.md, referenced from QUICK-START.md and UPGRADING.md. No separate README section.' README pointer is a one-line blockquote under Step 4, not a section.
narratives.Test: tests/content/test_readme_brownfield.py::TestBrownfieldGuide -- 9 assertions covering file existence, install options, migrate:vbrief mention + idempotency, source-of-truth/rendered-view semantics, pre-cutover detection guard + sentinel, post-migration task check, #397 ingestion, QUICK-START cross-reference, RFC2119 legend.
narratives.Outcome: Brownfield users have a single-page authoritative guide to add Deft to an existing project, avoiding the footguns of direct .md edits and mid-migration state drift.

items.title: docs/BROWNFIELD.md with 7 sections (install, migrate, semantics, guard, checklist, troubleshoot, related)
Link from QUICK-START.md Step 2 Case C and Step 3 note
One-line brownfield pointer from README Step 4 (no full section)
Content tests asserting required sections and cross-references
